tree.setup: Your phone rings. It's your friend Xiao Peng, who has the unusual habit of calling instead of texting. He insists that calling is 'more personal.' He always calls at exactly 6 PM because that's when he starts thinking about dinner, which is his favorite subject.
tree.setup_zh: 手机响了。是你朋友小鹏，他有一个奇怪的习惯——打电话不发消息。他坚持说打电话「更有人情味」。他总在晚上六点打，因为那时候他开始想晚饭，这是他最爱的话题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: wéi! shì wǒ, xiǎo péng! nǐ chī le méi?
turns.text_en: Hey! It's me, Xiao Peng! Have you eaten?

options.text_pinyin: hái méi ne! nǐ xiǎng chī shénme?
options.text_en: Not yet! What do you want to eat?
options.feedback: Xiao Peng is thrilled -- you've given him permission to talk about food.「我想吃火锅！你觉得怎么样？」还没呢 is natural casual Chinese for 'not yet.'
Xiao Peng is undeterred.「那你陪我去吃？我一个人吃没意思。」'Have you eaten?' is as much greeting as question in Chinese.

options.text_zh: 你怎么又打电话？发消息不行吗？
options.text_pinyin: nǐ zěnme yòu dǎ diànhuà? fā xiāoxi bù xíng ma?
options.text_en: Why are you calling again? Can't you text?
options.feedback: Xiao Peng sighs dramatically.「打电话多好啊，能听到声音！」He will never change. 又 means 'again' -- implies mild exasperation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我们去吃火锅吧！新开了一家，听说很好吃。七点在地铁站见？
turns.text_pinyin: wǒmen qù chī huǒguō ba! xīn kāi le yì jiā, tīngshuō hěn hǎochī. qī diǎn zài dìtiě zhàn jiàn?
turns.text_en: Let's go eat hotpot! A new place opened, I heard it's great. Meet at the subway at 7?

options.feedback: Xiao Peng hangs up immediately -- the deal is sealed and he needs to get ready. Quick confirmation + time = perfect phone plan.

options.text_zh: 七点太早了，七点半行不行？
options.text_pinyin: qī diǎn tài zǎo le, qī diǎn bàn xíng bu xíng?
options.feedback: Xiao Peng agrees reluctantly -- thirty minutes is a long time when you're hungry.「行行行，七点半！」行不行 is the casual way to ask 'OK or not?'

options.text_pinyin: huǒguō a... kěyǐ chī bié de ma?
options.text_en: Hotpot... can we eat something else?
options.feedback: A long pause on the line. Xiao Peng is processing the betrayal.「可以……那你想吃什么？」He'll recover, but hotpot was his first love.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 太好了！别迟到啊！我先到了就先点菜！
turns.text_pinyin: tài hǎo le! bié chídào a! wǒ xiān dào le jiù xiān diǎn cài!
turns.text_en: Great! Don't be late! If I get there first I'm ordering ahead!

tree.cultural_note: 你吃了没 (nǐ chī le méi, 'have you eaten?') is one of the most common greetings in Chinese -- it's a way of showing care, not necessarily asking about food. Friends often make plans by phone right before the event, especially for dinner.
